description: UI: the ticket dashboard shows `Move to Board` when at least one ticket is selected and the user can update tickets.
UI: the ticket dashboard does not expose an actionable `Move to Board` control when the user lacks update permission.
UI: opening bulk move shows the selected ticket summary and destination board and status controls.
UI: choosing a destination board reloads the status picker with only statuses valid for that board.
UI: choosing a destination board preselects that board's default status.
UI: the user can replace the preselected default with another valid destination-board status before confirming.
UI: confirmation stays disabled until a destination board is selected.
UI: confirmation stays disabled and the dialog explains the problem when the selected destination board has no valid statuses.
Integration DB-backed: bulk move updates a ticket's `board_id` and uses the destination board's default status when no override is provided.
Integration DB-backed: bulk move uses the explicitly selected destination status when the user overrides the default.
Integration DB-backed: bulk move clears `category_id` and `subcategory_id` for successfully moved tickets.
Integration DB-backed: bulk move rejects a destination status that does not belong to the chosen destination board.
Integration DB-backed: bulk move returns partial success when one selected ticket can be moved and another fails validation.
Integration DB-backed: bulk move returns per-ticket permission failures instead of moving unauthorized tickets.
UI: partial-success bulk move keeps only failed tickets selected and shows per-ticket failure messages in the dialog.
UI: full-success bulk move closes the dialog and clears the dashboard selection.
UI: after successful moves, the dashboard refreshes so moved tickets disappear when they no longer satisfy the active filters.
Regression: bulk delete behavior remains unchanged after adding the bulk move action to the dashboard header.
